description: Track and surface context-switch frequency from branch changes as a focus metric. Branch switches already recorded in git_activity (event_type='branch_switch', branch=destination, source_branch=NULL currently). Surface: 'You switched branches 14 times today across 5 repos' with implied ~23min focus cost per switch.

userStories.title: Populate source_branch on branch_switch events
userStories.description: As a user, I want branch_switch events to record the source branch (where I switched FROM) so switch pairs can be analyzed.
userStories.context: In git_ops.rs poll_repo(), branch_switch is recorded when state.last_head_branch != current_branch. The source_branch column exists in the schema (added in migration M2) but is passed as None for branch_switch events. state.last_head_branch holds the previous branch name at detection time.
acceptanceCriteria: branch_switch INSERT passes state.last_head_branch.as_deref() as source_branch instead of None
existing branch_switch rows with NULL source_branch are unaffected (no migration needed)
unit test: poll_repo() on a repo that has switched branches records non-NULL source_branch

userStories.title: DB query: fetch branch_switch events for a time range
userStories.description: As the system, I need a query function that returns branch_switch events in [from, to], grouped by repo, with timestamps, branch, and source_branch.
userStories.context: query_activity() in query.rs already fetches all event_types from git_activity. A dedicated query function for branch_switch events allows the context-switch feature to remain decoupled from ActivitySummary.
acceptanceCriteria: fn query_branch_switches(conn, from, to) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<BranchSwitchEvent>> added to query.rs
BranchSwitchEvent struct: repo_path: String, from_branch: Option<String>, to_branch: Option<String>, timestamp: DateTime<Utc>
query returns events ordered by timestamp ASC
unit test: insert two branch_switch rows, query returns both with correct fields

userStories.title: Switch-filtering algorithm: suppress noise switches
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want automated git operations (rebase, merge tooling, stash pop sequences) counted as context switches. A rapid back-and-forth switch (A->B->A within <2 min) is noise, not a context switch.
userStories.context: poll_repo() fires on every poll interval (default 30s). A rebase-in-progress can emit A->detached->B->A in quick succession. Detached HEAD (None branch) transitions are not real context switches.
acceptanceCriteria: fn filter_noise_switches(events: &[BranchSwitchEvent], min_dwell_secs: u64) -> Vec<BranchSwitchEvent> added to query.rs
switches where to_branch is None (detached HEAD) are excluded
round-trip pairs (A->B->A) where total elapsed < min_dwell_secs (default 120) are collapsed to zero switches
same-branch re-checkout (from_branch == to_branch) excluded
unit tests: detached HEAD excluded, round-trip < 2min excluded, round-trip > 2min kept as 2 switches, same-branch excluded

userStories.title: Per-repo and cross-repo context-switch aggregation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see how many switches occurred per repo and in total so I can identify which repo is the biggest source of interruption.
userStories.context: ActivitySummary.repos is Vec<RepoSummary>. RepoSummary needs a branch_switches: usize field. ActivitySummary needs total_branch_switches: usize. Cross-repo switches (switching between repos entirely, tracked via directory_presence) are out of scope for this story.
acceptanceCriteria: RepoSummary gains field: branch_switches: usize
ActivitySummary gains field: total_branch_switches: usize
query_activity() populates branch_switches per repo using filter_noise_switches with default min_dwell_secs=120
total_branch_switches = sum of repo branch_switches
unit test: two repos with switches, assert per-repo and total counts

userStories.title: Display context-switch signal in pretty output
userStories.description: As a user, when I run `blackbox today`, I want to see a context-switch line in the summary that quantifies distraction and its estimated focus cost.
userStories.context: render_summary_to_string() in output.rs builds the pretty summary. The summary line currently reads 'N commits, R reviews... across M repos (~Xh Ym)'. The focus cost estimate uses the Gloria Mark research baseline: 23 min to regain deep focus after each switch.
acceptanceCriteria: If total_branch_switches > 0, summary line appends ', N branch switches (~Xm focus cost)' where X = switches * 23
Per-repo block shows switch count if repo.branch_switches > 0: '  ~ N branch switches (main->feat->main)'  — last 3 unique branches listed as breadcrumb, truncated with '...' if more
If total_branch_switches == 0, nothing added (no noise)
Unit test: summary with 3 switches shows '3 branch switches (~69m focus cost)'

userStories.title: Include branch-switch data in JSON and CSV output
userStories.description: As a user using --format json or --format csv, I want branch-switch data available for downstream tooling.
userStories.context: JsonSummary and JsonRepo in output.rs. CsvRow is a flat struct. branch_switch events are already included in events[] in JSON since they're ActivityEvents. Only summary-level counts need adding.
acceptanceCriteria: JsonSummary gains field: total_branch_switches: usize
JsonRepo gains field: branch_switches: usize
CsvRow: branch_switch events already included (event_type='branch_switch'); no schema change needed
Unit test: render_json on summary with switches includes correct counts

userStories.title: Standalone `blackbox focus` subcommand
userStories.description: As a user, I want `blackbox focus` to show a concise context-switch report without the full activity detail, so I can quickly check my focus health.
userStories.context: New Commands variant in cli.rs + match arm in main.rs. Reuses query_activity() output. Format: header line + per-repo breakdown of switch counts. Supports --week flag for weekly view.
acceptanceCriteria: Commands::Focus { week: bool } added to cli.rs
match arm in main.rs calls run_focus_query()

Output format:
  === Focus Report: Today ===
  14 branch switches across 5 repos (~322m focus cost)
  
  repo-a: 8 switches
  repo-b: 4 switches
  repo-c: 2 switches
If zero switches: 'No branch switches recorded. Clean focus day.'
--week flag uses week_range()
Integration test: blackbox focus runs without error

userStories.title: Include context-switch line in standup output
userStories.description: As a user, I want standup output to mention context switches if they exceeded a threshold so my standup notes reflect interruption cost.
userStories.context: render_standup() in output.rs. Only include if total_branch_switches >= 5 (below that it's not noteworthy for a standup).
acceptanceCriteria: render_standup() appends '- Context switches: N (est. ~Xm focus cost)' to Total summary line if total_branch_switches >= 5
Below threshold: no mention
Unit test: 6 switches appears in standup, 4 does not

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
